ayahs.textArabic: قَدۡ أَفۡلَحَ ٱلۡمُؤۡمِنُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qad aflaha almuminoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Certainly will the believers have succeeded:
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Successful indeed are the believers:

ayahs.textArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ هُمۡ فِي صَلَاتِهِمۡ خَٰشِعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Allatheena hum fee salatihim khashiAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They who are during their prayer humbly submissive
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: those who humble themselves in prayer;

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ هُمۡ عَنِ ٱللَّغۡوِ مُعۡرِضُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallatheena hum AAani allaghwi muAAridoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they who turn away from ill speech
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: those who avoid idle talk;

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ هُمۡ لِلزَّكَوٰةِ فَٰعِلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallatheena hum lilzzakati faAAiloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they who are observant of zakah
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: those who pay alms-tax;

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ هُمۡ لِفُرُوجِهِمۡ حَٰفِظُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallatheena hum lifuroojihim hafithoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they who guard their private parts
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: those who guard their chastity

ayahs.textArabic: إِلَّا عَلَىٰٓ أَزۡوَٰجِهِمۡ أَوۡ مَا مَلَكَتۡ أَيۡمَٰنُهُمۡ فَإِنَّهُمۡ غَيۡرُ مَلُومِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Illa AAala azwajihim aw ma malakat aymanuhum fainnahum ghayru maloomeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Except from their wives or those their right hands possess, for indeed, they will not be blamed -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: except with their wives or those ˹bondwomen˺ in their possession, for then they are free from blame,

ayahs.textArabic: فَمَنِ ٱبۡتَغَىٰ وَرَآءَ ذَٰلِكَ فَأُوْلَٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلۡعَادُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Famani ibtagha waraa thalika faolaika humu alAAadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But whoever seeks beyond that, then those are the transgressors -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: but whoever seeks beyond that are the transgressors;

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ هُمۡ لِأَمَٰنَٰتِهِمۡ وَعَهۡدِهِمۡ رَٰعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallatheena hum liamanatihim waAAahdihim raAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they who are to their trusts and their promises attentive
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹the believers are also˺ those who are true to their trusts and covenants;

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ هُمۡ عَلَىٰ صَلَوَٰتِهِمۡ يُحَافِظُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallatheena hum AAala salawatihim yuhafithoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they who carefully maintain their prayers -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and those who are ˹properly˺ observant of their prayers.

ayahs.textArabic: أُوْلَٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلۡوَٰرِثُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Olaika humu alwarithoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Those are the inheritors
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: These are the ones who will be awarded

ayahs.textArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ يَرِثُونَ ٱلۡفِرۡدَوۡسَ هُمۡ فِيهَا خَٰلِدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Allatheena yarithoona alfirdawsa hum feeha khalidoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Who will inherit al-Firdaus. They will abide therein eternally.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Paradise as their own. They will be there forever.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ خَلَقۡنَا ٱلۡإِنسَٰنَ مِن سُلَٰلَةٖ مِّن طِينٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad khalaqna alinsana min sulalatin min teenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And certainly did We create man from an extract of clay.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And indeed, We created humankind from an extract of clay,

ayahs.textArabic: ثُمَّ جَعَلۡنَٰهُ نُطۡفَةٗ فِي قَرَارٖ مَّكِينٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thumma jaAAalnahu nutfatan fee qararin makeenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then We placed him as a sperm-drop in a firm lodging.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then placed each ˹human˺ as a sperm-drop in a secure place,

ayahs.textArabic: ثُمَّ خَلَقۡنَا ٱلنُّطۡفَةَ عَلَقَةٗ فَخَلَقۡنَا ٱلۡعَلَقَةَ مُضۡغَةٗ فَخَلَقۡنَا ٱلۡمُضۡغَةَ عِظَٰمٗا فَكَسَوۡنَا ٱلۡعِظَٰمَ لَحۡمٗا ثُمَّ أَنشَأۡنَٰهُ خَلۡقًا ءَاخَرَۚ فَتَبَارَكَ ٱللَّهُ أَحۡسَنُ ٱلۡخَٰلِقِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thumma khalaqna alnnutfata AAalaqatan fakhalaqna alAAalaqata mudghatan fakhalaqna almudghata AAithaman fakasawna alAAithama lahman thumma anshanahu khalqan akhara fatabaraka Allahu ahsanu alkhaliqeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then We made the sperm-drop into a clinging clot, and We made the clot into a lump [of flesh], and We made [from] the lump, bones, and We covered the bones with flesh; then We developed him into another creation. So blessed is Allah, the best of creators.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then We developed the drop into a clinging clot ˹of blood˺, then developed the clot into a lump ˹of flesh˺, then developed the lump into bones, then clothed the bones with flesh, then We brought it into being as a new creation. So Blessed is Allah, the Best of Creators.

ayahs.textArabic: ثُمَّ إِنَّكُم بَعۡدَ ذَٰلِكَ لَمَيِّتُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thumma innakum baAAda thalika lamayyitoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then indeed, after that you are to die.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: After that you will surely die,

ayahs.textArabic: ثُمَّ إِنَّكُمۡ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡقِيَٰمَةِ تُبۡعَثُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thumma innakum yawma alqiyamati tubAAathoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then indeed you, on the Day of Resurrection, will be resurrected.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then on the Day of Judgment you will be resurrected.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ خَلَقۡنَا فَوۡقَكُمۡ سَبۡعَ طَرَآئِقَ وَمَا كُنَّا عَنِ ٱلۡخَلۡقِ غَٰفِلِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad khalaqna fawqakum sabAAa taraiqa wama kunna AAani alkhalqi ghafileena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We have created above you seven layered heavens, and never have We been of [Our] creation unaware.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And indeed, We created above you seven levels ˹of heaven˺. We are never unmindful of ˹Our˺ creation.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَنزَلۡنَا مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءَۢ بِقَدَرٖ فَأَسۡكَنَّٰهُ فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِۖ وَإِنَّا عَلَىٰ ذَهَابِۭ بِهِۦ لَقَٰدِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waanzalna mina alssamai maan biqadarin faaskannahu fee alardi wainna AAala thahabin bihi laqadiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We have sent down rain from the sky in a measured amount and settled it in the earth. And indeed, We are Able to take it away.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We send down rain from the sky in perfect measure, causing it to soak into the earth. And We are surely able to take it away.

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَنشَأۡنَا لَكُم بِهِۦ جَنَّٰتٖ مِّن نَّخِيلٖ وَأَعۡنَٰبٖ لَّكُمۡ فِيهَا فَوَٰكِهُ كَثِيرَةٞ وَمِنۡهَا تَأۡكُلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faanshana lakum bihi jannatin min nakheelin waaAAnabin lakum feeha fawakihu katheeratun waminha takuloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We brought forth for you thereby gardens of palm trees and grapevines in which for you are abundant fruits and from which you eat.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: With it We produce for you gardens of palm trees and grapevines, in which there are abundant fruits, and from which you may eat,

ayahs.textArabic: وَشَجَرَةٗ تَخۡرُجُ مِن طُورِ سَيۡنَآءَ تَنۢبُتُ بِٱلدُّهۡنِ وَصِبۡغٖ لِّلۡأٓكِلِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Washajaratan takhruju min toori saynaa tanbutu bialdduhni wasibghin lilakileena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [We brought forth] a tree issuing from Mount Sinai which produces oil and food for those who eat.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: as well as ˹olive˺ trees which grow at Mount Sinai, providing oil and a condiment to eat.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِنَّ لَكُمۡ فِي ٱلۡأَنۡعَٰمِ لَعِبۡرَةٗۖ نُّسۡقِيكُم مِّمَّا فِي بُطُونِهَا وَلَكُمۡ فِيهَا مَنَٰفِعُ كَثِيرَةٞ وَمِنۡهَا تَأۡكُلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wainna lakum fee alanAAami laAAibratan nusqeekum mimma fee butooniha walakum feeha manafiAAu katheeratun waminha takuloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, for you in livestock is a lesson. We give you drink from that which is in their bellies, and for you in them are numerous benefits, and from them you eat.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And there is certainly a lesson for you in cattle, from whose bellies We give you ˹milk˺ to drink, and in them are many other benefits for you, and from them you may eat.

ayahs.textArabic: وَعَلَيۡهَا وَعَلَى ٱلۡفُلۡكِ تُحۡمَلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WaAAalayha waAAala alfulki tuhmaloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And upon them and on ships you are carried.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And you are carried upon ˹some of˺ them and upon ships.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ أَرۡسَلۡنَا نُوحًا إِلَىٰ قَوۡمِهِۦ فَقَالَ يَٰقَوۡمِ ٱعۡبُدُواْ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنۡ إِلَٰهٍ غَيۡرُهُۥٓۚ أَفَلَا تَتَّقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad arsalna noohan ila qawmihi faqala ya qawmi oAAbudoo Allaha ma lakum min ilahin ghayruhu afala tattaqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We had certainly sent Noah to his people, and he said, "O my people, worship Allah; you have no deity other than Him; then will you not fear Him?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, We sent Noah to his people. He declared, “O my people! Worship Allah ˹alone˺. You have no god other than Him. Will you not then fear ˹Him˺?”

ayahs.textArabic: فَقَالَ ٱلۡمَلَؤُاْ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ مِن قَوۡمِهِۦ مَا هَٰذَآ إِلَّا بَشَرٞ مِّثۡلُكُمۡ يُرِيدُ أَن يَتَفَضَّلَ عَلَيۡكُمۡ وَلَوۡ شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ لَأَنزَلَ مَلَٰٓئِكَةٗ مَّا سَمِعۡنَا بِهَٰذَا فِيٓ ءَابَآئِنَا ٱلۡأَوَّلِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faqala almalao allatheena kafaroo min qawmihi ma hatha illa basharun mithlukum yureedu an yatafaddala AAalaykum walaw shaa Allahu laanzala malaikatan ma samiAAna bihatha fee abaina alawwaleena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But the eminent among those who disbelieved from his people said, "This is not but a man like yourselves who wishes to take precedence over you; and if Allah had willed [to send a messenger], He would have sent down angels. We have not heard of this among our forefathers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But the disbelieving chiefs of his people said ˹to the masses˺, “This is only a human like you, who wants to be superior to you. Had Allah willed, He could have easily sent down angels ˹instead˺. We have never heard of this in ˹the history of˺ our forefathers.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنۡ هُوَ إِلَّا رَجُلُۢ بِهِۦ جِنَّةٞ فَتَرَبَّصُواْ بِهِۦ حَتَّىٰ حِينٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: In huwa illa rajulun bihi jinnatun fatarabbasoo bihi hatta heenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He is not but a man possessed with madness, so wait concerning him for a time."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He is simply insane, so bear with him for a while.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ رَبِّ ٱنصُرۡنِي بِمَا كَذَّبُونِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala rabbi onsurnee bima kaththabooni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Noah] said, "My Lord, support me because they have denied me."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Noah prayed, “My Lord! Help me, because they have denied ˹me˺.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَوۡحَيۡنَآ إِلَيۡهِ أَنِ ٱصۡنَعِ ٱلۡفُلۡكَ بِأَعۡيُنِنَا وَوَحۡيِنَا فَإِذَا جَآءَ أَمۡرُنَا وَفَارَ ٱلتَّنُّورُ فَٱسۡلُكۡ فِيهَا مِن كُلّٖ زَوۡجَيۡنِ ٱثۡنَيۡنِ وَأَهۡلَكَ إِلَّا مَن سَبَقَ عَلَيۡهِ ٱلۡقَوۡلُ مِنۡهُمۡۖ وَلَا تُخَٰطِبۡنِي فِي ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوٓاْ إِنَّهُم مُّغۡرَقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faawhayna ilayhi ani isnaAAi alfulka biaAAyunina wawahyina faitha jaa amruna wafara alttannooru faosluk feeha min kullin zawjayni ithnayni waahlaka illa man sabaqa AAalayhi alqawlu minhum wala tukhatibnee fee allatheena thalamoo innahum mughraqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So We inspired to him, "Construct the ship under Our observation, and Our inspiration, and when Our command comes and the oven overflows, put into the ship from each [creature] two mates and your family, except those for whom the decree [of destruction] has proceeded. And do not address Me concerning those who have wronged; indeed, they are to be drowned.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So We inspired him: “Build the Ark under Our ˹watchful˺ Eyes and directions. Then when Our command comes and the oven bursts ˹with water˺, take on board a pair from every species along with your family—except those against whom the decree ˹to drown˺ has already been passed. And do not plead with Me for those who have done wrong, for they will surely be drowned.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَإِذَا ٱسۡتَوَيۡتَ أَنتَ وَمَن مَّعَكَ عَلَى ٱلۡفُلۡكِ فَقُلِ ٱلۡحَمۡدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِي نَجَّىٰنَا مِنَ ٱلۡقَوۡمِ ٱلظَّٰلِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faitha istawayta anta waman maAAaka AAala alfulki faquli alhamdu lillahi allathee najjana mina alqawmi alththalimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when you have boarded the ship, you and those with you, then say, 'Praise to Allah who has saved us from the wrongdoing people.'
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then when you and those with you have settled in the Ark, say, “All praise is for Allah, Who saved us from the wrongdoing people.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَقُل رَّبِّ أَنزِلۡنِي مُنزَلٗا مُّبَارَكٗا وَأَنتَ خَيۡرُ ٱلۡمُنزِلِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqul rabbi anzilnee munzalan mubarakan waanta khayru almunzileena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And say, 'My Lord, let me land at a blessed landing place, and You are the best to accommodate [us].' "
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And pray, “My Lord! Allow me a blessed landing, for You are the best accommodator.”

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَأٓيَٰتٖ وَإِن كُنَّا لَمُبۡتَلِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna fee thalika laayatin wain kunna lamubtaleena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed in that are signs, and indeed, We are ever testing [Our servants].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely in this are lessons. And We ˹always˺ put ˹people˺ to the test.

ayahs.textArabic: ثُمَّ أَنشَأۡنَا مِنۢ بَعۡدِهِمۡ قَرۡنًا ءَاخَرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thumma anshana min baAAdihim qarnan akhareena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then We produced after them a generation of others.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then We raised another generation after them,

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَرۡسَلۡنَا فِيهِمۡ رَسُولٗا مِّنۡهُمۡ أَنِ ٱعۡبُدُواْ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنۡ إِلَٰهٍ غَيۡرُهُۥٓۚ أَفَلَا تَتَّقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faarsalna feehim rasoolan minhum ani oAAbudoo Allaha ma lakum min ilahin ghayruhu afala tattaqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We sent among them a messenger from themselves, [saying], "Worship Allah; you have no deity other than Him; then will you not fear Him?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and sent to them a messenger from among themselves, ˹declaring,˺ “Worship Allah ˹alone˺. You have no god other than Him. Will you not then fear ˹Him˺?”

ayahs.textArabic: وَقَالَ ٱلۡمَلَأُ مِن قَوۡمِهِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ وَكَذَّبُواْ بِلِقَآءِ ٱلۡأٓخِرَةِ وَأَتۡرَفۡنَٰهُمۡ فِي ٱلۡحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنۡيَا مَا هَٰذَآ إِلَّا بَشَرٞ مِّثۡلُكُمۡ يَأۡكُلُ مِمَّا تَأۡكُلُونَ مِنۡهُ وَيَشۡرَبُ مِمَّا تَشۡرَبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqala almalao min qawmihi allatheena kafaroo wakaththaboo biliqai alakhirati waatrafnahum fee alhayati alddunya ma hatha illa basharun mithlukum yakulu mimma takuloona minhu wayashrabu mimma tashraboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the eminent among his people who disbelieved and denied the meeting of the Hereafter while We had given them luxury in the worldly life said, "This is not but a man like yourselves. He eats of that from which you eat and drinks of what you drink.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But the chiefs of his people—who disbelieved, denied the meeting ˹with Allah˺ in the Hereafter, and were spoiled by the worldly luxuries We had provided for them—said ˹to the masses˺, “This is only a human like you. He eats what you eat, and drinks what you drink.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَئِنۡ أَطَعۡتُم بَشَرٗا مِّثۡلَكُمۡ إِنَّكُمۡ إِذٗا لَّخَٰسِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walain ataAAtum basharan mithlakum innakum ithan lakhasiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And if you should obey a man like yourselves, indeed, you would then be losers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And if you ˹ever˺ obey a human like yourselves, then you would certainly be losers.

ayahs.textArabic: أَيَعِدُكُمۡ أَنَّكُمۡ إِذَا مِتُّمۡ وَكُنتُمۡ تُرَابٗا وَعِظَٰمًا أَنَّكُم مُّخۡرَجُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: AyaAAidukum annakum itha mittum wakuntum turaban waAAithaman annakum mukhrajoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Does he promise you that when you have died and become dust and bones that you will be brought forth [once more]?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Does he promise you that once you are dead and reduced to dust and bones, you will be brought forth ˹alive˺?

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ هَيۡهَاتَ هَيۡهَاتَ لِمَا تُوعَدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hayhata hayhata lima tooAAadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: How far, how far, is that which you are promised.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Impossible, simply impossible is what you are promised!

ayahs.textArabic: إِنۡ هِيَ إِلَّا حَيَاتُنَا ٱلدُّنۡيَا نَمُوتُ وَنَحۡيَا وَمَا نَحۡنُ بِمَبۡعُوثِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: In hiya illa hayatuna alddunya namootu wanahya wama nahnu bimabAAootheena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Life is not but our worldly life - we die and live, but we will not be resurrected.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: There is nothing beyond our worldly life. We die, others are born, and none will be resurrected.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنۡ هُوَ إِلَّا رَجُلٌ ٱفۡتَرَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبٗا وَمَا نَحۡنُ لَهُۥ بِمُؤۡمِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: In huwa illa rajulun iftara AAala Allahi kathiban wama nahnu lahu bimumineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He is not but a man who has invented a lie about Allah, and we will not believe him."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He is no more than a man who has fabricated a lie about Allah, and we will never believe in him.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ رَبِّ ٱنصُرۡنِي بِمَا كَذَّبُونِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala rabbi onsurnee bima kaththabooni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "My Lord, support me because they have denied me."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The messenger prayed, “My Lord! Help me, because they have denied ˹me˺.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ عَمَّا قَلِيلٖ لَّيُصۡبِحُنَّ نَٰدِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala AAamma qaleelin layusbihunna nadimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Allah] said, "After a little, they will surely become regretful."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Allah responded, “Soon they will be truly regretful.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَخَذَتۡهُمُ ٱلصَّيۡحَةُ بِٱلۡحَقِّ فَجَعَلۡنَٰهُمۡ غُثَآءٗۚ فَبُعۡدٗا لِّلۡقَوۡمِ ٱلظَّٰلِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faakhathathumu alssayhatu bialhaqqi fajaAAalnahum ghuthaan fabuAAdan lilqawmi alththalimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So the shriek seized them in truth, and We made them as [plant] stubble. Then away with the wrongdoing people.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then the ˹mighty˺ blast overtook them with justice, and We reduced them to rubble. So away with the wrongdoing people!

ayahs.textArabic: ثُمَّ أَنشَأۡنَا مِنۢ بَعۡدِهِمۡ قُرُونًا ءَاخَرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thumma anshana min baAAdihim quroonan akhareena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then We produced after them other generations.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then We raised other generations after them.

ayahs.textArabic: مَا تَسۡبِقُ مِنۡ أُمَّةٍ أَجَلَهَا وَمَا يَسۡتَـٔۡخِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ma tasbiqu min ommatin ajalaha wama yastakhiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No nation will precede its time [of termination], nor will they remain [thereafter].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: No people can advance their doom, nor can they delay it.

ayahs.textArabic: ثُمَّ أَرۡسَلۡنَا رُسُلَنَا تَتۡرَاۖ كُلَّ مَا جَآءَ أُمَّةٗ رَّسُولُهَا كَذَّبُوهُۖ فَأَتۡبَعۡنَا بَعۡضَهُم بَعۡضٗا وَجَعَلۡنَٰهُمۡ أَحَادِيثَۚ فَبُعۡدٗا لِّقَوۡمٖ لَّا يُؤۡمِنُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thumma arsalna rusulana tatra kulla ma jaa ommatan rasooluha kaththaboohu faatbaAAna baAAdahum baAAdan wajaAAalnahum ahadeetha fabuAAdan liqawmin la yuminoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then We sent Our messengers in succession. Every time there came to a nation its messenger, they denied him, so We made them follow one another [to destruction], and We made them narrations. So away with a people who do not believe.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then We sent Our messengers in succession: whenever a messenger came to his people, they denied him. So We destroyed them, one after the other, reducing them to ˹cautionary˺ tales. So away with the people who refuse to believe!

ayahs.textArabic: ثُمَّ أَرۡسَلۡنَا مُوسَىٰ وَأَخَاهُ هَٰرُونَ بِـَٔايَٰتِنَا وَسُلۡطَٰنٖ مُّبِينٍ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thumma arsalna moosa waakhahu haroona biayatina wasultanin mubeenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then We sent Moses and his brother Aaron with Our signs and a clear authority
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then We sent Moses and his brother Aaron with Our signs and compelling proof

ayahs.textArabic: إِلَىٰ فِرۡعَوۡنَ وَمَلَإِيْهِۦ فَٱسۡتَكۡبَرُواْ وَكَانُواْ قَوۡمًا عَالِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ila firAAawna wamalaihi faistakbaroo wakanoo qawman AAaleena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: To Pharaoh and his establishment, but they were arrogant and were a haughty people.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: to Pharaoh and his chiefs, but they behaved arrogantly and were a tyrannical people.

ayahs.textArabic: فَقَالُوٓاْ أَنُؤۡمِنُ لِبَشَرَيۡنِ مِثۡلِنَا وَقَوۡمُهُمَا لَنَا عَٰبِدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faqaloo anuminu libasharayni mithlina waqawmuhuma lana AAabidoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "Should we believe two men like ourselves while their people are for us in servitude?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They argued, “Will we believe in two humans, like ourselves, whose people are slaves to us?”

ayahs.textArabic: فَكَذَّبُوهُمَا فَكَانُواْ مِنَ ٱلۡمُهۡلَكِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fakaththaboohuma fakanoo mina almuhlakeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So they denied them and were of those destroyed.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So they rejected them both, and ˹so˺ were among those destroyed.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ ءَاتَيۡنَا مُوسَى ٱلۡكِتَٰبَ لَعَلَّهُمۡ يَهۡتَدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad atayna moosa alkitaba laAAallahum yahtadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We certainly gave Moses the Scripture that perhaps they would be guided.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We certainly gave Moses the Scripture, so perhaps his people would be ˹rightly˺ guided.

ayahs.textArabic: وَجَعَلۡنَا ٱبۡنَ مَرۡيَمَ وَأُمَّهُۥٓ ءَايَةٗ وَءَاوَيۡنَٰهُمَآ إِلَىٰ رَبۡوَةٖ ذَاتِ قَرَارٖ وَمَعِينٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WajaAAalna ibna maryama waommahu ayatan waawaynahuma ila rabwatin thati qararin wamaAAeenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We made the son of Mary and his mother a sign and sheltered them within a high ground having level [areas] and flowing water.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We made the son of Mary and his mother a sign, and gave them refuge on high ground—a ˹suitable˺ place for rest with flowing water.

ayahs.textArabic: يَٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلرُّسُلُ كُلُواْ مِنَ ٱلطَّيِّبَٰتِ وَٱعۡمَلُواْ صَٰلِحًاۖ إِنِّي بِمَا تَعۡمَلُونَ عَلِيمٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ya ayyuha alrrusulu kuloo mina alttayyibati waiAAmaloo salihan innee bima taAAmaloona AAaleemun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Allah said], "O messengers, eat from the good foods and work righteousness. Indeed, I, of what you do, am Knowing.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: O messengers! Eat from what is good and lawful, and act righteously. Indeed, I fully know what you do.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِنَّ هَٰذِهِۦٓ أُمَّتُكُمۡ أُمَّةٗ وَٰحِدَةٗ وَأَنَا۠ رَبُّكُمۡ فَٱتَّقُونِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wainna hathihi ommatukum ommatan wahidatan waana rabbukum faittaqooni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed this, your religion, is one religion, and I am your Lord, so fear Me."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely this religion of yours is ˹only˺ one, and I am your Lord, so fear Me ˹alone˺.

ayahs.textArabic: فَتَقَطَّعُوٓاْ أَمۡرَهُم بَيۡنَهُمۡ زُبُرٗاۖ كُلُّ حِزۡبِۭ بِمَا لَدَيۡهِمۡ فَرِحُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: FataqattaAAoo amrahum baynahum zuburan kullu hizbin bima ladayhim farihoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But the people divided their religion among them into sects - each faction, in what it has, rejoicing.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Yet the people have divided it into different sects, each rejoicing in what they have.

ayahs.textArabic: فَذَرۡهُمۡ فِي غَمۡرَتِهِمۡ حَتَّىٰ حِينٍ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fatharhum fee ghamratihim hatta heenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So leave them in their confusion for a time.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So leave them ˹O Prophet˺ in their heedlessness for a while.

ayahs.textArabic: أَيَحۡسَبُونَ أَنَّمَا نُمِدُّهُم بِهِۦ مِن مَّالٖ وَبَنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ayahsaboona annama numidduhum bihi min malin wabaneena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Do they think that what We extend to them of wealth and children
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Do they think, since We provide them with wealth and children,

ayahs.textArabic: نُسَارِعُ لَهُمۡ فِي ٱلۡخَيۡرَٰتِۚ بَل لَّا يَشۡعُرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: NusariAAu lahum fee alkhayrati bal la yashAAuroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Is [because] We hasten for them good things? Rather, they do not perceive.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: that We hasten to ˹honour˺ them ˹with˺ all kinds of good? No! They are not aware.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ هُم مِّنۡ خَشۡيَةِ رَبِّهِم مُّشۡفِقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna allatheena hum min khashyati rabbihim mushfiqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, they who are apprehensive from fear of their Lord
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely those who tremble in awe of their Lord,

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ هُم بِـَٔايَٰتِ رَبِّهِمۡ يُؤۡمِنُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallatheena hum biayati rabbihim yuminoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they who believe in the signs of their Lord
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and who believe in the revelations of their Lord,

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ هُم بِرَبِّهِمۡ لَا يُشۡرِكُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallatheena hum birabbihim la yushrikoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they who do not associate anything with their Lord
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and who associate none with their Lord,

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ يُؤۡتُونَ مَآ ءَاتَواْ وَّقُلُوبُهُمۡ وَجِلَةٌ أَنَّهُمۡ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِمۡ رَٰجِعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallatheena yutoona ma ataw waquloobuhum wajilatun annahum ila rabbihim rajiAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they who give what they give while their hearts are fearful because they will be returning to their Lord -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and who do whatever ˹good˺ they do with their hearts fearful, ˹knowing˺ that they will return to their Lord—

ayahs.textArabic: أُوْلَٰٓئِكَ يُسَٰرِعُونَ فِي ٱلۡخَيۡرَٰتِ وَهُمۡ لَهَا سَٰبِقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Olaika yusariAAoona fee alkhayrati wahum laha sabiqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: It is those who hasten to good deeds, and they outstrip [others] therein.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: it is they who race to do good deeds, always taking the lead.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَا نُكَلِّفُ نَفۡسًا إِلَّا وُسۡعَهَاۚ وَلَدَيۡنَا كِتَٰبٞ يَنطِقُ بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَهُمۡ لَا يُظۡلَمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wala nukallifu nafsan illa wusAAaha waladayna kitabun yantiqu bialhaqqi wahum la yuthlamoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We charge no soul except [with that within] its capacity, and with Us is a record which speaks with truth; and they will not be wronged.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We never require of any soul more than what it can afford. And with Us is a record which speaks the truth. None will be wronged.

ayahs.textArabic: بَلۡ قُلُوبُهُمۡ فِي غَمۡرَةٖ مِّنۡ هَٰذَا وَلَهُمۡ أَعۡمَٰلٞ مِّن دُونِ ذَٰلِكَ هُمۡ لَهَا عَٰمِلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Bal quloobuhum fee ghamratin min hatha walahum aAAmalun min dooni thalika hum laha AAamiloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But their hearts are covered with confusion over this, and they have [evil] deeds besides disbelief which they are doing,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But the hearts of those ˹who disbelieve˺ are oblivious to ˹all of˺ this, and they have other ˹evil˺ deeds, opposite to this, in which they are engrossed.

ayahs.textArabic: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَآ أَخَذۡنَا مُتۡرَفِيهِم بِٱلۡعَذَابِ إِذَا هُمۡ يَجۡـَٔرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hatta itha akhathna mutrafeehim bialAAathabi itha hum yajaroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Until when We seize their affluent ones with punishment, at once they are crying [to Allah] for help.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But as soon as We seize their elite with torment, they start to cry for help.

ayahs.textArabic: لَا تَجۡـَٔرُواْ ٱلۡيَوۡمَۖ إِنَّكُم مِّنَّا لَا تُنصَرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: La tajaroo alyawma innakum minna la tunsaroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Do not cry out today. Indeed, by Us you will not be helped.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They will be told,˺ “Do not cry for help today. Surely you will never be saved from Us.

ayahs.textArabic: قَدۡ كَانَتۡ ءَايَٰتِي تُتۡلَىٰ عَلَيۡكُمۡ فَكُنتُمۡ عَلَىٰٓ أَعۡقَٰبِكُمۡ تَنكِصُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qad kanat ayatee tutla AAalaykum fakuntum AAala aAAqabikum tankisoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: My verses had already been recited to you, but you were turning back on your heels
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, My revelations were recited to you, but you used to back away ˹in disgust˺,

ayahs.textArabic: مُسۡتَكۡبِرِينَ بِهِۦ سَٰمِرٗا تَهۡجُرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Mustakbireena bihi samiran tahjuroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: In arrogance regarding it, conversing by night, speaking evil.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: boasting of the Sacred House, and babbling ˹nonsense about the Quran˺ by night.”

ayahs.textArabic: أَفَلَمۡ يَدَّبَّرُواْ ٱلۡقَوۡلَ أَمۡ جَآءَهُم مَّا لَمۡ يَأۡتِ ءَابَآءَهُمُ ٱلۡأَوَّلِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Afalam yaddabbaroo alqawla am jaahum ma lam yati abaahumu alawwaleena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then have they not reflected over the Qur'an, or has there come to them that which had not come to their forefathers?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Is it because they have never contemplated the Word ˹of Allah˺? Or ˹because˺ there has come to them something that did not come to their forefathers?

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ لَمۡ يَعۡرِفُواْ رَسُولَهُمۡ فَهُمۡ لَهُۥ مُنكِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am lam yaAArifoo rasoolahum fahum lahu munkiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or did they not know their Messenger, so they are toward him disacknowledging?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or ˹because˺ they failed to recognize their Messenger, and so they denied him?

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ يَقُولُونَ بِهِۦ جِنَّةُۢۚ بَلۡ جَآءَهُم بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَأَكۡثَرُهُمۡ لِلۡحَقِّ كَٰرِهُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am yaqooloona bihi jinnatun bal jaahum bialhaqqi waaktharuhum lilhaqqi karihoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or do they say, "In him is madness?" Rather, he brought them the truth, but most of them, to the truth, are averse.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or ˹because˺ they say, “He is insane?” In fact, he has come to them with the truth, but most of them are resentful of the truth.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَوِ ٱتَّبَعَ ٱلۡحَقُّ أَهۡوَآءَهُمۡ لَفَسَدَتِ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتُ وَٱلۡأَرۡضُ وَمَن فِيهِنَّۚ بَلۡ أَتَيۡنَٰهُم بِذِكۡرِهِمۡ فَهُمۡ عَن ذِكۡرِهِم مُّعۡرِضُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walawi ittabaAAa alhaqqu ahwaahum lafasadati alssamawatu waalardu waman feehinna bal ataynahum bithikrihim fahum AAan thikrihim muAAridoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But if the Truth had followed their inclinations, the heavens and the earth and whoever is in them would have been ruined. Rather, We have brought them their message, but they, from their message, are turning away.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Had the truth followed their desires, the heavens, the earth, and all those in them would have certainly been corrupted. In fact, We have brought them ˹the means to˺ their glory, but they turn away from it.

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ تَسۡـَٔلُهُمۡ خَرۡجٗا فَخَرَاجُ رَبِّكَ خَيۡرٞۖ وَهُوَ خَيۡرُ ٱلرَّٰزِقِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am tasaluhum kharjan fakharaju rabbika khayrun wahuwa khayru alrraziqeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or do you, [O Muhammad], ask them for payment? But the reward of your Lord is best, and He is the best of providers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or ˹is it because˺ you ˹O Prophet˺ are asking them for tribute? But the reward of your Lord is best, for He is the Best Provider.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِنَّكَ لَتَدۡعُوهُمۡ إِلَىٰ صِرَٰطٖ مُّسۡتَقِيمٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wainnaka latadAAoohum ila siratin mustaqeemin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, you invite them to a straight path.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And surely you are calling them to the Straight Path,

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤۡمِنُونَ بِٱلۡأٓخِرَةِ عَنِ ٱلصِّرَٰطِ لَنَٰكِبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wainna allatheena la yuminoona bialakhirati AAani alssirati lanakiboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But indeed, those who do not believe in the Hereafter are deviating from the path.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: but those who disbelieve in the Hereafter are certainly deviating from that Path.

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ وَلَوۡ رَحِمۡنَٰهُمۡ وَكَشَفۡنَا مَا بِهِم مِّن ضُرّٖ لَّلَجُّواْ فِي طُغۡيَٰنِهِمۡ يَعۡمَهُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaw rahimnahum wakashafna ma bihim min durrin lalajjoo fee tughyanihim yaAAmahoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And even if We gave them mercy and removed what was upon them of affliction, they would persist in their transgression, wandering blindly.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Even˺ if We had mercy on them and removed their affliction, they would still persist in their transgression, wandering blindly.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ أَخَذۡنَٰهُم بِٱلۡعَذَابِ فَمَا ٱسۡتَكَانُواْ لِرَبِّهِمۡ وَمَا يَتَضَرَّعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad akhathnahum bialAAathabi fama istakanoo lirabbihim wama yatadarraAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We had gripped them with suffering [as a warning], but they did not yield to their Lord, nor did they humbly supplicate, [and will continue thus]
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We have already seized them with torment, but they never humbled themselves to their Lord, nor did they ˹submissively˺ appeal ˹to Him˺.

ayahs.textArabic: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا فَتَحۡنَا عَلَيۡهِم بَابٗا ذَا عَذَابٖ شَدِيدٍ إِذَا هُمۡ فِيهِ مُبۡلِسُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hatta itha fatahna AAalayhim baban tha AAathabin shadeedin itha hum feehi mublisoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Until when We have opened before them a door of severe punishment, immediately they will be therein in despair.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But as soon as We open for them a gate of severe punishment, they will be utterly desperate.

ayahs.textArabic: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِيٓ أَنشَأَ لَكُمُ ٱلسَّمۡعَ وَٱلۡأَبۡصَٰرَ وَٱلۡأَفۡـِٔدَةَۚ قَلِيلٗا مَّا تَشۡكُرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wahuwa allathee anshaa lakumu alssamAAa waalabsara waalafidata qaleelan ma tashkuroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And it is He who produced for you hearing and vision and hearts; little are you grateful.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He is the One Who created for you hearing, sight, and intellect. ˹Yet˺ you hardly give any thanks.

ayahs.textArabic: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِي ذَرَأَكُمۡ فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَإِلَيۡهِ تُحۡشَرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wahuwa allathee tharaakum fee alardi wailayhi tuhsharoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And it is He who has multiplied you throughout the earth, and to Him you will be gathered.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And He is the One Who has dispersed you ˹all˺ over the earth, and to Him you will ˹all˺ be gathered.

ayahs.textArabic: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِي يُحۡيِۦ وَيُمِيتُ وَلَهُ ٱخۡتِلَٰفُ ٱلَّيۡلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِۚ أَفَلَا تَعۡقِلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wahuwa allathee yuhyee wayumeetu walahu ikhtilafu allayli waalnnahari afala taAAqiloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And it is He who gives life and causes death, and His is the alternation of the night and the day. Then will you not reason?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And He is the One Who gives life and causes death, and to Him belongs the alternation of the day and night. Will you not then understand?

ayahs.textArabic: بَلۡ قَالُواْ مِثۡلَ مَا قَالَ ٱلۡأَوَّلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Bal qaloo mithla ma qala alawwaloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Rather, they say like what the former peoples said.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But they ˹just˺ say what their predecessors said.

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُوٓاْ أَءِذَا مِتۡنَا وَكُنَّا تُرَابٗا وَعِظَٰمًا أَءِنَّا لَمَبۡعُوثُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo aitha mitna wakunna turaban waAAithaman ainna lamabAAoothoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "When we have died and become dust and bones, are we indeed to be resurrected?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They said, “Once we are dead and reduced to dust and bones, will we really be resurrected?

ayahs.textArabic: لَقَدۡ وُعِدۡنَا نَحۡنُ وَءَابَآؤُنَا هَٰذَا مِن قَبۡلُ إِنۡ هَٰذَآ إِلَّآ أَسَٰطِيرُ ٱلۡأَوَّلِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Laqad wuAAidna nahnu waabaona hatha min qablu in hatha illa asateeru alawwaleena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: We have been promised this, we and our forefathers, before; this is not but legends of the former peoples."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We have already been promised this, as well as our forefathers earlier. This is nothing but ancient fables!”

ayahs.textArabic: قُل لِّمَنِ ٱلۡأَرۡضُ وَمَن فِيهَآ إِن كُنتُمۡ تَعۡلَمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul limani alardu waman feeha in kuntum taAAlamoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, [O Muhammad], "To whom belongs the earth and whoever is in it, if you should know?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Ask ˹them, O Prophet˺, “To whom belong the earth and all those on it, if you ˹really˺ know?”

ayahs.textArabic: سَيَقُولُونَ لِلَّهِۚ قُلۡ أَفَلَا تَذَكَّرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Sayaqooloona lillahi qul afala tathakkaroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will say, "To Allah." Say, "Then will you not remember?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will reply, “To Allah!” Say, “Why are you not then mindful?”

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡ مَن رَّبُّ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ ٱلسَّبۡعِ وَرَبُّ ٱلۡعَرۡشِ ٱلۡعَظِيمِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul man rabbu alssamawati alssabAAi warabbu alAAarshi alAAatheemi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, "Who is Lord of the seven heavens and Lord of the Great Throne?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹And˺ ask ˹them˺, “Who is the Lord of the seven heavens and the Lord of the Mighty Throne?”

ayahs.textArabic: سَيَقُولُونَ لِلَّهِۚ قُلۡ أَفَلَا تَتَّقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Sayaqooloona lillahi qul afala tattaqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will say, "[They belong] to Allah." Say, "Then will you not fear Him?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will reply, “Allah.” Say, “Will you not then fear ˹Him˺?”

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡ مَنۢ بِيَدِهِۦ مَلَكُوتُ كُلِّ شَيۡءٖ وَهُوَ يُجِيرُ وَلَا يُجَارُ عَلَيۡهِ إِن كُنتُمۡ تَعۡلَمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul man biyadihi malakootu kulli shayin wahuwa yujeeru wala yujaru AAalayhi in kuntum taAAlamoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, "In whose hand is the realm of all things - and He protects while none can protect against Him - if you should know?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Ask ˹them also,˺ “In Whose Hands is the authority over all things, protecting ˹all˺ while none can protect against Him, if you ˹really˺ know?”

ayahs.textArabic: سَيَقُولُونَ لِلَّهِۚ قُلۡ فَأَنَّىٰ تُسۡحَرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Sayaqooloona lillahi qul faanna tusharoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will say, "[All belongs] to Allah." Say, "Then how are you deluded?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will reply, “Allah.” Say, “How are you then so deluded?”

ayahs.textArabic: بَلۡ أَتَيۡنَٰهُم بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَإِنَّهُمۡ لَكَٰذِبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Bal ataynahum bialhaqqi wainnahum lakathiboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Rather, We have brought them the truth, and indeed they are liars.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In fact, We have brought them the truth, and they are certainly liars.

ayahs.textArabic: مَا ٱتَّخَذَ ٱللَّهُ مِن وَلَدٖ وَمَا كَانَ مَعَهُۥ مِنۡ إِلَٰهٍۚ إِذٗا لَّذَهَبَ كُلُّ إِلَٰهِۭ بِمَا خَلَقَ وَلَعَلَا بَعۡضُهُمۡ عَلَىٰ بَعۡضٖۚ سُبۡحَٰنَ ٱللَّهِ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ma ittakhatha Allahu min waladin wama kana maAAahu min ilahin ithan lathahaba kullu ilahin bima khalaqa walaAAala baAAduhum AAala baAAdin subhana Allahi AAamma yasifoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Allah has not taken any son, nor has there ever been with Him any deity. [If there had been], then each deity would have taken what it created, and some of them would have sought to overcome others. Exalted is Allah above what they describe [concerning Him].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Allah has never had ˹any˺ offspring, nor is there any god besides Him. Otherwise, each god would have taken away what he created, and they would have tried to dominate one another. Glorified is Allah above what they claim!

ayahs.textArabic: عَٰلِمِ ٱلۡغَيۡبِ وَٱلشَّهَٰدَةِ فَتَعَٰلَىٰ عَمَّا يُشۡرِكُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: AAalimi alghaybi waalshshahadati fataAAala AAamma yushrikoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [He is] Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, so high is He above what they associate [with Him].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹He is the˺ Knower of the seen and unseen. Exalted is He above what they associate ˹with Him˺.

ayahs.textArabic: قُل رَّبِّ إِمَّا تُرِيَنِّي مَا يُوعَدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul rabbi imma turiyannee ma yooAAadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, [O Muhammad], "My Lord, if You should show me that which they are promised,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “My Lord! Should You show me what they are threatened with,

ayahs.textArabic: رَبِّ فَلَا تَجۡعَلۡنِي فِي ٱلۡقَوۡمِ ٱلظَّٰلِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Rabbi fala tajAAalnee fee alqawmi alththalimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: My Lord, then do not place me among the wrongdoing people."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then, my Lord, do not count me among the wrongdoing people.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِنَّا عَلَىٰٓ أَن نُّرِيَكَ مَا نَعِدُهُمۡ لَقَٰدِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wainna AAala an nuriyaka ma naAAiduhum laqadiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, We are able to show you what We have promised them.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We are indeed able to show you what We have threatened them with.

ayahs.textArabic: ٱدۡفَعۡ بِٱلَّتِي هِيَ أَحۡسَنُ ٱلسَّيِّئَةَۚ نَحۡنُ أَعۡلَمُ بِمَا يَصِفُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: IdfaAA biallatee hiya ahsanu alssayyiata nahnu aAAlamu bima yasifoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Repel, by [means of] what is best, [their] evil. We are most knowing of what they describe.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Respond to evil with what is best. We know well what they claim.

ayahs.textArabic: وَقُل رَّبِّ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنۡ هَمَزَٰتِ ٱلشَّيَٰطِينِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqul rabbi aAAoothu bika min hamazati alshshayateeni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And say, "My Lord, I seek refuge in You from the incitements of the devils,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And say, “My Lord! I seek refuge in You from the temptations of the devils.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ رَبِّ أَن يَحۡضُرُونِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WaaAAoothu bika rabbi an yahdurooni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And I seek refuge in You, my Lord, lest they be present with me."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And I seek refuge in You, my Lord, that they ˹even˺ come near me.”

ayahs.textArabic: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءَ أَحَدَهُمُ ٱلۡمَوۡتُ قَالَ رَبِّ ٱرۡجِعُونِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hatta itha jaa ahadahumu almawtu qala rabbi irjiAAooni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [For such is the state of the disbelievers], until, when death comes to one of them, he says, "My Lord, send me back
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: When death approaches any of them, they cry, “My Lord! Let me go back,

ayahs.textArabic: لَعَلِّيٓ أَعۡمَلُ صَٰلِحٗا فِيمَا تَرَكۡتُۚ كـَلَّآۚ إِنَّهَا كَلِمَةٌ هُوَ قَآئِلُهَاۖ وَمِن وَرَآئِهِم بَرۡزَخٌ إِلَىٰ يَوۡمِ يُبۡعَثُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: LaAAallee aAAmalu salihan feema taraktu kalla innaha kalimatun huwa qailuha wamin waraihim barzakhun ila yawmi yubAAathoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That I might do righteousness in that which I left behind." No! It is only a word he is saying; and behind them is a barrier until the Day they are resurrected.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: so I may do good in what I left behind.” Never! It is only a ˹useless˺ appeal they make. And there is a barrier behind them until the Day they are resurrected.

ayahs.textArabic: فَإِذَا نُفِخَ فِي ٱلصُّورِ فَلَآ أَنسَابَ بَيۡنَهُمۡ يَوۡمَئِذٖ وَلَا يَتَسَآءَلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faitha nufikha fee alssoori fala ansaba baynahum yawmaithin wala yatasaaloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So when the Horn is blown, no relationship will there be among them that Day, nor will they ask about one another.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then, when the Trumpet will be blown, there will be no kinship between them on that Day, nor will they ˹even care to˺ ask about one another.

ayahs.textArabic: فَمَن ثَقُلَتۡ مَوَٰزِينُهُۥ فَأُوْلَٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلۡمُفۡلِحُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faman thaqulat mawazeenuhu faolaika humu almuflihoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those whose scales are heavy [with good deeds] - it is they who are the successful.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: As for those whose scale is heavy ˹with good deeds˺, it is they who will be successful.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَنۡ خَفَّتۡ مَوَٰزِينُهُۥ فَأُوْلَٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ خَسِرُوٓاْ أَنفُسَهُمۡ فِي جَهَنَّمَ خَٰلِدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waman khaffat mawazeenuhu faolaika allatheena khasiroo anfusahum fee jahannama khalidoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But those whose scales are light - those are the ones who have lost their souls, [being] in Hell, abiding eternally.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But those whose scale is light, they will have doomed themselves, staying in Hell forever.

ayahs.textArabic: تَلۡفَحُ وُجُوهَهُمُ ٱلنَّارُ وَهُمۡ فِيهَا كَٰلِحُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Talfahu wujoohahumu alnnaru wahum feeha kalihoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Fire will sear their faces, and they therein will have taut smiles.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The Fire will burn their faces, leaving them deformed.

ayahs.textArabic: أَلَمۡ تَكُنۡ ءَايَٰتِي تُتۡلَىٰ عَلَيۡكُمۡ فَكُنتُم بِهَا تُكَذِّبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Alam takun ayatee tutla AAalaykum fakuntum biha tukaththiboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [It will be said]. "Were not My verses recited to you and you used to deny them?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹It will be said,˺ “Were My revelations not recited to you, but you used to deny them?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ رَبَّنَا غَلَبَتۡ عَلَيۡنَا شِقۡوَتُنَا وَكُنَّا قَوۡمٗا ضَآلِّينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo rabbana ghalabat AAalayna shiqwatuna wakunna qawman dalleena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will say, "Our Lord, our wretchedness overcame us, and we were a people astray.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will cry, “Our Lord! Our ill-fate took hold of us, so we became a misguided people.

ayahs.textArabic: رَبَّنَآ أَخۡرِجۡنَا مِنۡهَا فَإِنۡ عُدۡنَا فَإِنَّا ظَٰلِمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Rabbana akhrijna minha fain AAudna fainna thalimoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Our Lord, remove us from it, and if we were to return [to evil], we would indeed be wrongdoers."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Our Lord! Take us out of this ˹Fire˺. Then if we ever return ˹to denial˺, we will truly be wrongdoers.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ ٱخۡسَـُٔواْ فِيهَا وَلَا تُكَلِّمُونِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala ikhsaoo feeha wala tukallimooni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He will say, "Remain despised therein and do not speak to Me.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Allah will respond, “Be despised in there! Do not ˹ever˺ plead with Me ˹again˺!

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ فَرِيقٞ مِّنۡ عِبَادِي يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَآ ءَامَنَّا فَٱغۡفِرۡ لَنَا وَٱرۡحَمۡنَا وَأَنتَ خَيۡرُ ٱلرَّٰحِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Innahu kana fareequn min AAibadee yaqooloona rabbana amanna faighfir lana wairhamna waanta khayru alrrahimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, there was a party of My servants who said, 'Our Lord, we have believed, so forgive us and have mercy upon us, and You are the best of the merciful.'
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, there was a group of My servants who used to pray, ‘Our Lord! We have believed, so forgive us and have mercy on us, for You are the best of those who show mercy,’

ayahs.textArabic: فَٱتَّخَذۡتُمُوهُمۡ سِخۡرِيًّا حَتَّىٰٓ أَنسَوۡكُمۡ ذِكۡرِي وَكُنتُم مِّنۡهُمۡ تَضۡحَكُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faittakhathtumoohum sikhriyyan hatta ansawkum thikree wakuntum minhum tadhakoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But you took them in mockery to the point that they made you forget My remembrance, and you used to laugh at them.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: but you were ˹so busy˺ making fun of them that it made you forget My remembrance. And you used to laugh at them.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنِّي جَزَيۡتُهُمُ ٱلۡيَوۡمَ بِمَا صَبَرُوٓاْ أَنَّهُمۡ هُمُ ٱلۡفَآئِزُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Innee jazaytuhumu alyawma bima sabaroo annahum humu alfaizoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, I have rewarded them this Day for their patient endurance - that they are the attainers [of success]."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Today I have indeed rewarded them for their perseverance: they are certainly the triumphant.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَٰلَ كَمۡ لَبِثۡتُمۡ فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ عَدَدَ سِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala kam labithtum fee alardi AAadada sineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Allah] will say, "How long did you remain on earth in number of years?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He will ask ˹them˺, “How many years did you remain on earth?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ لَبِثۡنَا يَوۡمًا أَوۡ بَعۡضَ يَوۡمٖ فَسۡـَٔلِ ٱلۡعَآدِّينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo labithna yawman aw baAAda yawmin faisali alAAaddeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will say, "We remained a day or part of a day; ask those who enumerate."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will reply, “We remained ˹only˺ a day or part of a day. But ask those who kept count.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَٰلَ إِن لَّبِثۡتُمۡ إِلَّا قَلِيلٗاۖ لَّوۡ أَنَّكُمۡ كُنتُمۡ تَعۡلَمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala in labithtum illa qaleelan law annakum kuntum taAAlamoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He will say, "You stayed not but a little - if only you had known.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He will say, “You only remained for a little while, if only you knew.

ayahs.textArabic: أَفَحَسِبۡتُمۡ أَنَّمَا خَلَقۡنَٰكُمۡ عَبَثٗا وَأَنَّكُمۡ إِلَيۡنَا لَا تُرۡجَعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Afahasibtum annama khalaqnakum AAabathan waannakum ilayna la turjaAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then did you think that We created you uselessly and that to Us you would not be returned?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Did you then think that We had created you without purpose, and that you would never be returned to Us?”

ayahs.textArabic: فَتَعَٰلَى ٱللَّهُ ٱلۡمَلِكُ ٱلۡحَقُّۖ لَآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ رَبُّ ٱلۡعَرۡشِ ٱلۡكَرِيمِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: FataAAala Allahu almaliku alhaqqu la ilaha illa huwa rabbu alAAarshi alkareemi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So exalted is Allah, the Sovereign, the Truth; there is no deity except Him, Lord of the Noble Throne.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Exalted is Allah, the True King! There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Him, the Lord of the Honourable Throne.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَن يَدۡعُ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا ءَاخَرَ لَا بُرۡهَٰنَ لَهُۥ بِهِۦ فَإِنَّمَا حِسَابُهُۥ عِندَ رَبِّهِۦٓۚ إِنَّهُۥ لَا يُفۡلِحُ ٱلۡكَٰفِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waman yadAAu maAAa Allahi ilahan akhara la burhana lahu bihi fainnama hisabuhu AAinda rabbihi innahu la yuflihu alkafiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And whoever invokes besides Allah another deity for which he has no proof - then his account is only with his Lord. Indeed, the disbelievers will not succeed.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Whoever invokes, besides Allah, another god—for which they can have no proof—they will surely find their penalty with their Lord. Indeed, the disbelievers will never succeed.

ayahs.textArabic: وَقُل رَّبِّ ٱغۡفِرۡ وَٱرۡحَمۡ وَأَنتَ خَيۡرُ ٱلرَّٰحِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqul rabbi ighfir wairham waanta khayru alrrahimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And, [O Muhammad], say, "My Lord, forgive and have mercy, and You are the best of the merciful."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “My Lord! Forgive and have mercy, for You are the best of those who show mercy.”
